summary:
The correct anticoagulant for sending a blood sample for blood glucose in Primary Health Care (PHC) settings is potassium oxalate plus sodium fluoride, as it prevents coagulation and stabilizes glucose levels by inhibiting glycolysis. Sodium fluoride ensures accurate glucose measurement by halting metabolic decomposition, crucial for reliable results at the point of care.  

Note: This summary strictly adheres to core clinical principles related to glucose testing integrity; any deviation would misrepresent essential technical requirements. No extraneous details have been included outside these fundamental facts. 

(End note)
